content.problemStatement:
Value of an array is defined as the sum of the absolute differences between pairs of consecutive elements in the array. Formally, for a given array A={A1,A2,A3,…,An}A = \{A_1, A_2, A_3, \dots, A_n\}A={A1​,A2​,A3​,…,An​},

value(A)=∣A1−A2∣+∣A2−A3∣+∣A3−A4∣+⋯+∣An−1−An∣\text{value}(A) = |A_1 - A_2| + |A_2 - A_3| + |A_3 - A_4| + \dots + |A_{n-1} - A_n|value(A)=∣A1​−A2​∣+∣A2​−A3​∣+∣A3​−A4​∣+⋯+∣An−1​−An​∣where ∣x∣|x|∣x∣ means the absolute value of xxx.

You will be given an array AAA of nnn integers and qqq updates. Each update will have two integers xxx and yyy. For this update, you should replace all the occurrences of element xxx in the array with yyy and output the value of the new array.


Input Format
Input starts with an integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.


The first line of each case contains two integers nnn and qqq. The next line contains nnn space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,…,AnA_1, A_2, A_3, \dots, A_nA1​,A2​,A3​,…,An​ forming the initial array.


Each of the next qqq lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy.



Output Format
For each test case, print `"Case t:"` (without quotes, where ttt is the test case number) in the first line. Then print qqq lines. The ithi^{th}ith line should contain the value of the array after the ithi^{th}ith update.
content.inputFormat: The input starts with an integer T, denoting the number of test cases. Each test case begins with two integers n and q, where n is the number of elements in the array and q is the number of updates. The next line contains n space-separated integers A1, A2, A3, …, An forming the initial array. Each of the next q lines contains two space-separated integers x and y, representing an update where all occurrences of x in the array are replaced with y.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'Case t:' (without quotes, where t is the test case number) in the first line. Then print q lines, each containing the value of the array after the corresponding update.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: After the first update, A = {3, 2, 3, 4, 5}, Value(A) = |3-2| + |2-3| + |3-4| + |4-5| = 4. After the second update, A = {4, 2, 4, 4, 5}, Value(A) = |4-2| + |2-4| + |4-4| + |4-5| = 5. After the third update, A = {4, 2, 4, 4, 1}, Value(A) = |4-2| + |2-4| + |4-4| + |4-1| = 7.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first calculate the initial value of the array by summing the absolute differences between consecutive elements. Then, for each update, we replace all occurrences of x with y in the array and recalculate the value of the array. We use a simple iterative approach to calculate the value of the array after each update.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (n + q * n)) — The time complexity is dominated by the loop over all test cases (T), the initial calculation of the array value (n), and the updates (q * n), where n is the number of elements in the array and q is the number of updates.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — The space complexity is dominated by the storage of the array of n elements.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
for _ in range(int(input())):
    n, q = map(int, input().split())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    print(f"Case {_ + 1}:")
    for _ in range(q):
        x, y = map(int, input().split())
        A = [y if a == x else a for a in A]
        value = sum(abs(A[i] - A[i + 1]) for i in range(n - 1))
        print(value)
